title: Context Propagation, Deadlines, OS Signals, and Graceful Shutdown · Execution Trace: Pokedex Go Context Tree
summary: Handlers create background contexts, Ctrl-C exits before cleanup, signal notifications are never stopped, blocked input and fetch work outlive the session, and cancellation is reported as failure. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go context tree increment in signal-aware graceful command lifecycle. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Propagate caller context through session, handler, loader, transport, cache wait, and persistence without storing it in long-lived structs.
Create one root context with signal.NotifyContext, call its stop function, and define portable interrupt behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Predict Pokedex Go Context Tree · Theory Pokedex Go Context Tree
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 4869 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove root signal cancellation, context propagation, worker completion, and idempotent cleanup; failed and canceled commands must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
